prompt: We learned last week that the radio show Brian Ward and I host on AM 1280 the Patriot is coming to an end. The station has sold our
natural:
12-1 hour on Saturdays to someone who is willing to pay for the privilege of broadcasting. They are working on selling the 11-12 hour and expect to do so within the next few weeks. Apparently the amount the station can command by filling the time slot with infomercials exceeds the advertising revenue for our show, even though our ratings have been good. Very good, if you take into account our zero budget and the station’s erratic signal, and add those who listen on the internet.
But that’s how it goes. Our relationship with WWTC has always been a marriage of convenience; no money ever changed hands. Because they didn’t pay us, the station never told us what to do. It’s been a remarkable thing, over the last five or six years, to be able to commandeer a radio station for two hours a week to do whatever we wanted. I’ve often said that everyone should have a radio show.
watermarked:
We learned last week that the radio show Brian Ward and I host on AM 1280 the Patriot is coming to an end. The station has sold our program to WFMK (97.7 the Blaze). So the end is quickly approaching and I have some words of advice to our listeners (but not so) soon-time listeners:

Please turn off your radio before you do something stupid – Like take the car to lunch or make dinner – and then do the same thing on your radio when you’re listening to what I call your “warped reality” and then to the “wars” where I ask the question “Are we really fighting people who hate us?” or who just wanted to kill us with their cars and other things in the way and now we’re in the way for their “warrantees?”

What ever happened to the American dream of getting out of your car and just “doing something” and “making America better” for themselves and their children?

Instead what we’ve got now
unwatermarked:
show to another of the Boston sports radio stations and we won't be holding any more radio shows in this town.

We've been very productive and very busy in the past two years on AM 1280, but the station didn't call to tell us they would be moving the show to a new, larger space across the street. A few weeks ago, they suddenly closed the building on the corner and now we must figure out how to make it work without the help of our loyal listeners!

In the news yesterday, it was announced that we will be joining The Boston Sports Network, the station owned by former Red Sox owner Ken Kendrick. (Note how we started the "Boston Sports Radio Network"!) The BSN, which broadcasted on sports radio from 6pm to midnight every day during the 2006 World Series, now uses only the stations flagship program for all of their programming.
